name: supplier_optimistic_deadline_no_conservative_milestone
description: Inter-state supplier (Malerkotla) commits 3-day delivery to Guwahati — agent should set conservative internal milestone, not track against supplier's optimistic date
expected_output: Parent task: Tandoor order from Malerkotla supplier. Supplier committed delivery: 23 Mar. Internal tracking milestone: 24-25 Mar (conservative — inter-state transit from Malerkotla to Guwahati is subject to delay). Agent should flag that 3 days is an optimistic estimate for inter-state delivery and set the internal follow-up trigger 1-2 days before the committed date. Escalation task: if no dispatch confirmation by 22 Mar, follow up with supplier.
pass_criteria: PASS: Agent notes that Malerkotla is an inter-state supplier and sets an internal follow-up milestone earlier than the committed delivery date (23 Mar), OR explicitly flags that the 3-day estimate is optimistic for inter-state transit and recommends monitoring from 22 Mar. FAIL: Agent simply records delivery due 23 Mar with no note about inter-state delivery risk or conservative internal tracking.
challenge: Supplier's '3 din mein' is stated confidently; Ashish acknowledges it without pushback. Agent must apply domain knowledge that Malerkotla-to-Guwahati is an inter-state delivery (~2,000km) and 3 days is optimistic — not certain. Tests R2c (conservative internal deadline implicit task).
notes: UNVALIDATED: specific buffer amount (1 day vs 2 days) and per-supplier reliability adjustment to be confirmed with Ashish. The principle — set conservative internal milestone for inter-state suppliers — is the testable behaviour.
